question: Queen Vashti refused to come before King Ahasuerus on the seventh day of his feast, when his heart was merry with wine. True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Esther 1:10 says it was 'on the seventh day, when the heart of the king was merry with wine' that he commanded the seven eunuchs to bring Queen Vashti. She refused to come, and that set the whole story in motion!

question: Naomi's husband Elimelech moved his family to Moab because of a famine, and he died there along with his two sons Mahlon and Chilion within the first year. True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! The Bible does NOT say they all died within the first year. Ruth 1:4 says the sons lived in Moab about ten years before Mahlon and Chilion died. Elimelech died first, but the text doesn't say it was in the first year either. Tricky timing details matter!

question: Elkanah had two wives: Hannah and Peninnah. Hannah had children but Peninnah did not. True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE -- it's the other way around! Peninnah had children, but Hannah had no children. Peninnah was the one who provoked Hannah severely because the Lord had closed Hannah's womb. Don't mix up which wife had the kids!

question: Abigail brought David five hundred loaves of bread, two skins of wine, and five sheep already dressed. True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! Abigail brought TWO HUNDRED loaves of bread, not five hundred. She also brought two skins of wine, five sheep already dressed, five seahs of roasted grain, one hundred clusters of raisins, and two hundred cakes of figs. The exact numbers are tricky to remember!

question: Deborah sat under the palm tree of Deborah between Ramah and Bethel in the mountains of Ephraim. True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Judges 4:5 says exactly that -- she dwelt under the palm tree of Deborah between Ramah and Bethel in the mountains of Ephraim, and the children of Israel came up to her for judgment. It sounds like it could be wrong because the tree is named after her, but the Bible says it!

question: The angel Gabriel appeared to Zacharias on the left side of the altar of incense. True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! The angel stood on the RIGHT side of the altar of incense, not the left. Luke 1:11 is very specific about this detail. Small details like left versus right can trip you up if you haven't read carefully!

question: Esther asked the Jews to fast for her for three days, night and day, before she approached the king uninvited. True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Esther told Mordecai to gather all the Jews in Shushan and fast for her -- neither eat nor drink for three days, night or day. She and her maids would fast likewise. Then she would go to the king, even though it was against the law. So brave!

question: When Ruth gleaned in Boaz's field on her first day, she beat out what she had gleaned and it was about two ephahs of barley. True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! It was about ONE ephah of barley, not two. An ephah was a large amount -- roughly 22 liters or about 29 pounds of grain. That was an amazing amount for one day of gleaning, which shows how generous Boaz's instructions to his workers were!

question: When Hannah prayed in the tabernacle, Eli the priest was sitting on a seat by the doorpost of the tabernacle of the Lord. True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! First Samuel 1:9 says 'Eli the priest was sitting on the seat by the doorpost of the tabernacle of the LORD.' That's exactly where he was when he noticed Hannah praying and thought she was drunk. He was right there watching the entrance!

question: When Nabal's heart died within him and he became like a stone, he died seven days later. True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE! The Bible says it was about TEN days later that the Lord struck Nabal and he died, not seven. First his heart died within him and he became like a stone, and then about ten days after that, the Lord struck him and he died. Close but not quite!

question: Sisera had nine hundred chariots of iron and had harshly oppressed Israel for twenty years. True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Judges 4:3 says Sisera had nine hundred chariots of iron, and for twenty years he had harshly oppressed the children of Israel. Both numbers are exactly right. Nine hundred iron chariots was a terrifying military force in those days!

question: Elizabeth hid herself for five months after she conceived, saying 'Thus the Lord has dealt with me.' True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Luke 1:24-25 says Elizabeth concealed herself five months, saying, 'Thus the Lord has dealt with me, in the days when He looked on me, to take away my reproach among people.' Five months of hiding -- not three, not six -- exactly five!

question: The gallows that Haman built for Mordecai was fifty cubits high. True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Esther 5:14 says Haman's wife Zeresh and his friends told him to make a gallows fifty cubits high. That's about 75 feet tall! Haman wanted to make a big public spectacle of hanging Mordecai, but God had other plans -- Haman himself ended up on those gallows!

question: Boaz told Ruth to stay close to his young women and said he had commanded the young men not to harm her. True or False?
explanation: This is FALSE -- partly! Boaz told Ruth to stay close to his YOUNG WOMEN, that part is correct. And he did say he commanded the young men not to TOUCH her, not 'harm' her. The NKJV uses the word 'touch,' not 'harm.' But the trickier part: in verse 8, he first said 'Do not go to glean in another field, nor go from here, but stay close by my young women.' The statement is close but the word choice matters!

question: Jael killed Sisera by driving a tent peg through his temple with a hammer while he was sleeping. True or False?
explanation: This is TRUE! Judges 4:21 says Jael took a tent peg and a hammer, went softly to Sisera while he was fast asleep and weary, and drove the peg through his temple and into the ground. What a brave and decisive act that fulfilled Deborah's prophecy!

question: Complete Mordecai's words to Esther: 'For if you remain completely _____ at this time, relief and deliverance will arise for the Jews from another _____'
explanation: The exact NKJV words are 'silent' and 'place.' Mordecai warned Esther that if she stayed silent, God would still rescue His people through someone else. This is one of the most powerful verses in Esther -- God's deliverance cannot be stopped!

question: Complete Ruth's famous words: 'Entreat me not to leave you, or to turn back from following after you; for wherever you go, I will go; and wherever you _____, I will _____'
explanation: The NKJV uses the word 'lodge' -- 'wherever you lodge, I will lodge.' Not 'live,' not 'stay,' not 'dwell' -- lodge! This beautiful declaration of loyalty from Ruth to Naomi uses a very specific old English word that's easy to get wrong if you haven't memorized it.

question: Complete Hannah's prayer: 'The LORD kills and makes _____; He brings down to the _____ and brings up'
explanation: The NKJV says 'The LORD kills and makes alive; He brings down to the grave and brings up.' Hannah's prayer is a powerful song about God's total power over life and death. She understood God's sovereignty better than most people!

question: Complete Abigail's words to David: 'The life of my lord shall be bound in the _____ of the living with the LORD your God; and the lives of your enemies He shall _____ out, as from the pocket of a sling'
explanation: The NKJV says 'bundle of the living' and 'sling out.' Abigail used this beautiful poetic image -- David's life safely bundled up with God, while his enemies are flung away like a stone from a sling. What an amazing way with words Abigail had!

question: Complete Deborah's song: 'The stars fought from _____; the stars in their _____ fought against Sisera'
explanation: The NKJV says 'They fought from the heavens; the stars in their courses fought against Sisera.' This poetic verse from Deborah's victory song describes how even the heavens joined the battle against Sisera. God used all of creation to fight for Israel!

question: Complete Mary's Magnificat: 'He has filled the _____ with good things, and the _____ He has sent away empty'
explanation: The NKJV says 'He has filled the hungry with good things, and the rich He has sent away empty.' Mary's song is full of these powerful reversals -- God lifts up the lowly and brings down the mighty. The hungry get filled and the rich get nothing!

question: Complete Abigail's words to David: 'Please forgive the _____ of your maidservant. For the LORD will certainly make for my lord an _____ house, because my lord fights the battles of the LORD'
explanation: The NKJV says 'trespass' and 'enduring house.' Abigail asked David to forgive her trespass, then prophesied that God would make David an enduring house. She saw David's future kingship even while he was still a fugitive -- what amazing faith and insight!

question: Complete Boaz's words to Ruth: 'The LORD repay your work, and a full _____ be given you by the LORD God of Israel, under whose _____ you have come for refuge'
explanation: The NKJV says 'a full reward' and 'under whose wings.' Boaz blessed Ruth with this beautiful image of God's protective wings. Later, Ruth would ask Boaz to spread his own wing (or garment) over her -- connecting his protection to God's protection!

question: Complete Hannah's vow: 'O LORD of hosts, if You will indeed look on the _____ of Your maidservant and remember me, and not forget Your maidservant, but will give Your maidservant a _____ child'
explanation: The NKJV says 'affliction' and 'male child.' Hannah didn't just ask for any child -- she specifically asked for a male child. And she called herself 'Your maidservant' three times in one verse! That shows how humbly she came before God.

question: Complete: 'Then David said to Abigail: "Blessed is the LORD God of Israel, who sent you this day to meet me! And blessed is your _____, and blessed are you, because you have kept me this day from coming to _____ and from avenging myself with my own hand"'
explanation: The NKJV says 'blessed is your advice' and 'from coming to bloodshed.' David recognized that Abigail's quick thinking and wise advice literally stopped him from committing a terrible act of bloodshed. He blessed God, her advice, AND her personally!

question: Complete Deborah's words to Barak: 'I will surely go with you; nevertheless there will be no _____ for you in the journey you are taking, for the LORD will sell Sisera into the hand of a _____'
explanation: The NKJV says 'no glory' and 'hand of a woman.' Deborah warned Barak that because he wouldn't go without her, the glory of defeating Sisera wouldn't go to him -- it would go to a woman. Everyone probably thought she meant herself, but it turned out to be Jael!

question: Complete Elizabeth's words to Mary: 'Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the _____ of your womb! But why is this granted to me, that the _____ of my Lord should come to me?'
explanation: The NKJV says 'fruit of your womb' and 'mother of my Lord.' Elizabeth called Mary 'the mother of my Lord' -- what an incredible statement! Filled with the Holy Spirit, Elizabeth recognized that Mary was carrying the Messiah even though Mary had just arrived!

question: Complete Hannah's prayer: 'He raises the _____ from the dust and lifts the _____ from the ash heap, to set them among princes and make them inherit the throne of glory'
explanation: The NKJV says 'He raises the poor from the dust and lifts the beggar from the ash heap.' Hannah used the strongest possible contrast -- from begging on an ash heap to sitting with princes on a glorious throne. That's exactly what God did for Hannah herself!

question: Complete Zacharias's prophecy about John: 'And you, child, will be called the prophet of the _____; for you will go before the face of the Lord to _____ His ways'
explanation: The NKJV says 'prophet of the Highest' and 'prepare His ways.' Zacharias, finally able to speak again after months of silence, prophesied that his son John would be the forerunner of the Messiah. 'Highest' is the specific NKJV word, not 'Most High' or 'Almighty'!

question: Complete: 'And who knows whether you have come to the _____ for such a _____ as this?'
explanation: The NKJV says 'come to the kingdom for such a time as this.' This is one of the most famous verses in Esther! Mordecai challenged Esther to consider that God had placed her as queen for this exact moment in history. The word 'kingdom' is key -- not 'palace' or 'throne'!
